_doc: Compiled from Guild_analysis_v3.md — FFA-weighted guild interpretation rules

Fiber Degraders.function: Degrade polysaccharides and resistant starch. Release oligosaccharides, acetate, propionate, succinate. Entry point of substrate cascade.
Fiber Degraders.critical_impact: Loss is the earliest structural failure
Fiber Degraders.system_role: Ecosystem Gatekeepers — determine whether ecosystem is diet-fed or host-fed
Fiber Degraders.client_description: fiber-processing bacteria that break down plant foods into fuel for other beneficial bacteria

HMO/Oligosaccharide-Utilising Bifidobact.function: Convert oligosaccharides to lactate and acetate via phosphoketolase pathway. Rapid acidification. Amplify carbohydrate fermentation.
HMO/Oligosaccharide-Utilising Bifidobact.critical_impact: Absence eliminates lactate production pathway, reducing SCFA amplification by 50-70%
HMO/Oligosaccharide-Utilising Bifidobact.system_role: Early Fermentation Amplifiers — generate intermediates for secondary fermenters
HMO/Oligosaccharide-Utilising Bifidobact.client_description: Bifidobacteria that convert plant sugars into lactate — a key fuel that feeds your gut-lining energy producers

Cross-Feeders.function: Consume lactate, acetate, succinate, hydrogen. Convert intermediates to propionate and butyrate precursors. Maintain redox balance.
Cross-Feeders.critical_impact: Absence creates metabolic dead ends — intermediates accumulate
Cross-Feeders.system_role: Secondary Fermentation Stabilizers — CRITICAL CONNECTORS between degradation and terminal SCFA
Cross-Feeders.client_description: intermediate processors that convert raw fermentation products into finished beneficial compounds, preventing waste buildup

Butyrate Producers.function: Produce butyrate from acetate, lactate, simple sugars. Feed colonocytes. Maintain epithelial barrier. Anti-inflammatory signaling.
Butyrate Producers.critical_impact: Depletion reduces colonocyte energy, barrier integrity, anti-inflammatory capacity
Butyrate Producers.system_role: Terminal SCFA Producers — downstream-dependent, require acetate supply
Butyrate Producers.client_description: bacteria that produce butyrate — the primary energy source for your gut lining cells, and a key anti-inflammatory compound

Mucin Degraders.function: Degrade mucin O-glycans. Produce acetate and propionate. Always-on backup carbon source.

interpretation_spectrum.too_low_0_1: Insufficient mucin turnover — accumulation of aged mucus
interpretation_spectrum.optimal_1_4: Healthy mucus homeostasis — balanced degradation and production
interpretation_spectrum.concerning_4_8: Elevated — context-dependent, concerning if low fiber
interpretation_spectrum.problematic_above_8: Excessive barrier erosion — fiber starvation likely

Mucin Degraders.system_role: Host-Substrate Users — adaptive buffer during fiber fluctuation, pathological when dominant
Mucin Degraders.client_description: bacteria that maintain your gut's protective mucus layer through controlled turnover

Proteolytic Dysbiosis Guild.function: Ferment amino acids and bile substrates. Produce ammonia, H2S, phenols, indoles, BCFAs. Expansion is downstream consequence of substrate shift.

dose_dependent.moderate_1_5: Normal — necessary for BCAA processing and nitrogen cycling

Proteolytic Dysbiosis Guild.system_role: Putrefactive Strategy — indicates substrate competition shifted toward protein
Proteolytic Dysbiosis Guild.client_description: protein-fermenting bacteria that produce harsher byproducts when they become too abundant

FCR.question: Are intermediates efficiently converted to terminal SCFAs?

efficient.label: Efficient conversion
efficient.meaning: Intermediates efficiently converted to terminal SCFA

moderate.label: Moderate efficiency
moderate.meaning: Adequate but not optimal intermediate processing

bottleneck_identification.bifido_absent: Without Bifidobacteria-derived lactate, the fermentation pathway lacks its primary intermediate fuel
bottleneck_identification.cross_feeders_low: Insufficient connector bacteria to process fermentation intermediates
bottleneck_identification.butyrate_low: Terminal processors depleted — intermediates produced but not converted to butyrate
bottleneck_identification.general: Fermentation intermediates accumulate rather than being converted to beneficial compounds

MDR.question: Is ecosystem diet-fed or relying on host substrate?

PPR.question: Is metabolism SCFA-dominant or protein fermentation-driven?

clr_interpretation.description: CLR value meaning — competitive position relative to sample geometric mean

nine_scenario_matrix.description: 9 interpretation scenarios from CLR (competitive status) × Evenness (structural resilience)

enriched_high_J.label: Optimal
enriched_high_J.interpretation: Adequate capacity + winning competition + robust redundancy

enriched_low_J.label: CRITICAL VULNERABILITY
enriched_low_J.interpretation: Severe overabundance concentrated in single species — monoculture
enriched_low_J.intervention: PRIORITY 1A — Reduce overabundance AND build redundancy

balanced_low_J.label: Hidden vulnerability
balanced_low_J.interpretation: Adequate total but concentrated — perturbation sensitive

depleted_high_J.label: Favorable for recovery
depleted_high_J.interpretation: Severe deficit BUT genetic reservoir maintained — multiple species ready to expand

depleted_low_J.label: WORST CASE
depleted_low_J.interpretation: Critical depletion with monoculture fragility — single-species dependency with no backup
depleted_low_J.intervention: CRITICAL — Triple strategy: maintain dominant + diversify + probiotic seed

multi_axis_synthesis.description: Integration of Axis1 (absolute capacity) + Axis2 (relative CLR competition)

within_range_depleted.meaning: Adequate capacity BUT losing competition = Underutilized

below_range_depleted.meaning: Insufficient capacity + losing competition = Critical deficit

components.CLR_Severity: Distance from guild-specific optimal CLR (0-10 scale)

priority_calculation.note: Dependency multipliers are heuristic prioritization weights, not empirically measured network impact ratios

undefined_clr_handling.rule: When guild CLR is undefined (abundance <1%), ALWAYS treat as 0 in ratio calculations. NEVER return 'undefined' as a ratio result.
undefined_clr_handling.rationale: Undefined CLR means guild absent/near-absent, effectively contributing 0 competitive weight
undefined_clr_handling.averaging_rule: For ratios combining guilds (CUR, FCR), ALWAYS average even if one guild is 0

fcr_conditional_interpretation.description: When Bifidobacteria absent (<1%), FCR interpretation changes
fcr_conditional_interpretation.bifido_absent: Modified: metabolic flux likely through lactate-independent pathways. Direct fiber→acetate/propionate→butyrate route. FCR value suggests metabolic routing rather than lactate efficiency.
fcr_conditional_interpretation.note: Model-based inference from genetic data, not direct metabolic flux measurement

extreme_clr_ratios.description: When ratios exceed ±2.0, indicates near-absence of one guild
extreme_clr_ratios.MDR_very_negative: Mucin Degraders nearly absent (<0.5%), extreme diet-feeding
extreme_clr_ratios.PPR_very_negative: Proteolytic nearly absent (<1%), extreme SCFA dominance
extreme_clr_ratios.CUR_very_positive: Proteolytic nearly absent, extreme carbohydrate preference
extreme_clr_ratios.reporting: Report extreme values as calculated — they accurately represent ecosystem state

fiber_side.description: Fiber → Guild1 breaks down → Guilds 2&4 convert (parallel) → Guild3 consolidates → SCFA pool

key_loops.bifido_butyrate: Bifidobacteria produce lactate → Roseburia/F.prausnitzii convert to butyrate (2-3× SCFA amplification)
key_loops.succinate_detox: Prevotella/Bacteroides produce succinate → Phascolarctobacterium converts to propionate
key_loops.hydrogen_management: H2 from fermenters → consumed by Methanobrevibacter(→methane), Blautia(→acetate), Anaerobutyricum

backup_line.description: Host mucin → Guild5 (Mucin Degraders) → acetate/propionate → feeds Guilds 3&4
backup_line.behavior: High fiber → Guild5 minimal (protective). Low fiber → Guild5 compensatory (adaptive short-term, problematic long-term)

protein_line.description: Dietary protein → Guild6 (Proteolytic) → ammonia, H2S, phenols, BCFAs
protein_line.impact: Damages epithelium, promotes inflammation, selects against butyrate producers, tips community away from SCFA

trophic_cascades.critical_dependency: Bifidobacteria absence → no lactate amplification → Cross-Feeders substrate-limited → butyrate production reduced 50-70% → single-pathway fermentation only

metabolic_network_model.description: Parallel processing model — NOT sequential. Guilds 2 (Bifido) and 4 (Cross-Feeders) operate simultaneously.
metabolic_network_model.flow: Fiber → Guild1(Fiber Degraders) → [Parallel: Guild2(Bifido) + Guild4(Cross-Feeders)] → Guild3(Butyrate Producers) → SCFA Pool
metabolic_network_model.backup: Guild5(Mucin) → always-on backup, increases when fiber decreases
metabolic_network_model.competing: Guild6(Proteolytic) → protein-driven alternative, produces inflammatory metabolites
metabolic_network_model.key_principle: Healthy gut = carbohydrate-driven ecosystem. Dysbiosis = protein and mucin-driven ecosystem.
